# CC4101 Tarea 2

## Parsing e Intérpretes

Para la resolución de la tarea recuerde que

                   Toda función debe estar acompañada de su firma, una breve descripción coloquial (en T2.rkt) y un conjunto significativo de tests (en test.rkt).
                   Todo datatype definido por el usuario (via deftype) debe estar acompañado de
                   una breve descripción coloquial y de la gramática BNF que lo genera.

              Si la función o el datatype no cumple con estas reglas, será ignorado.

         Ejercicio 1                                                                         22 Pt
            Se define un lenguaje aritmético cuyos programas denotan polinomios, en lugar de
            números. La sintaxis abstracta del lenguaje está dada por la siguiente gramática BNF:
               #|
               <poly> ::= (poly (Listof <number>))
                        | (id <id>)
                        | (add <poly> <poly>)
                        | (mul <poly> <poly>)
                        | (if0 <poly> <poly> <poly>)
                        | (with <id> <poly> <poly>)
               |#

               Un polinomio está representado por una lista de números, en donde cada posición
               contiene el coeficiente correspondiente a ese grado. Por ejemplo, (poly '(4 0 5)) representa al polinomio 4 + 5x2 .
               Dentro de las construcciones del lenguaje están los identificadores, la suma y el producto de polinomios, un condicional que compara la guarda con el polinomio nulo
               ((poly '(0))) y la definición local que asocia un identificador con un polinomio para
               que pueda ser referenciado en el cuerpo de la misma.
                (a) [6 Pt] Defina el tipo de datos recursivo Poly que genera los AST de los programas
                    del lenguaje.

                (b) [6 Pt] Defina la sintaxis concreta del lenguaje y escriba la función
                    ;; parse ::   <s-poly> -> Poly
                    que devuelve los programas escritos en sintaxis abstracta. Para ello, considere
                    los siguientes ejemplos:

        >>> (parse 0)
        (poly '(0))
        >>> (parse '(3 1 0 0))
        (poly '(3 1))
        >>> (parse 'x)
        (id 'x)
        >>> (parse '(+ (3 5 7) (1 2)))
        (add (poly '(3 5 7)) (poly '(1 2)))
        >>> (parse '(* (2 1) (1 2)))
        (mul (poly '(2 1)) (poly '(1 2)))
        >>> (parse '(if0 (+ (3 5) (-3 2)) 1 0))
        (if0 (add (poly '(3 5)) (poly '(-3 2))) (poly '(1)) (poly '(0)))
        >>> (parse '(with x (1 2) (* 2 x)))
        (with 'x (poly '(1 2)) (mul (poly '(2)) (id 'x)))

    (c) [10 Pt] Sea la estructura Env que modela un ambiente en donde se tienen las
        relaciones entre los identificadores y sus valores en forma de polinomios puros,
        es decir, sin operaciones y únicamente formado por el constructor poly. Defina
        la función
        ;; reduce ::   Poly Env -> Poly
        que devuelve un polinomio puro.
        >>> (reduce (poly '(3 1 0)) empty-env)
        (poly '(3 1))
        >>> (reduce (id 'x) empty-env)
        reduce: variable x is not defined
        >>> (reduce (id 'x) (extend-env x (poly '(1)) empty-env))
        (poly '(1))
        >>> (reduce (add (poly '(3 5 7)) (poly '(1 2))) empty-env)
        (poly '(4 7 7)

Ejercicio 2                                                                          38 Pt
   El archivo T2.rkt contiene una definición inicial del tipo de datos recursivo Expr, basado en el lenguaje visto en clases.
    (a) [6 Pt] Extienda Expr (y su gramática BNF) con operadores números reales e
        imaginarios. Además, agregue una forma de introducir varias definiciones locales
        en una sola expresión, es decir, una forma de with con varias definiciones. Para
        ello, utilice los siguientes ejemplos como guía (no es necesario implementar la
        función parser aún):
             Números reales e imaginarios:
        >>> (parser '1)
        ( real 1)
        >>> (parser '(1 i ))
        (imaginary 1)
        >>> (parser '(+ 2 (1 i )))
        (addc (real 2) (imaginary 1))
            Identificadores y definiciones locales:

    >>> (parser '(with [ (x 1) (y 1)] (+ x y)))
    (withc (list (cons 'x ( real 1)) (cons 'y ( real 1))) (addc (idc 'x) (idc 'y)))

(b) [6 Pt] Implemente la función parser siguiendo los ejemplos anteriores. Además,
    considere que la expresión with siempre debe recibir al menos una definición y
    en caso contrario se debe lanzar una excepción:
    >>> (parser '(with [ ] 1))
    parser: *with* expects at least one definition

(c) [6 Pt] En el archivo base, se encuentra una definición de valores CValue para
    el lenguaje. Dado que este trabaja con números reales e imaginarios, naturalmente los valores son los números complejos. Implemente la función from-CValue
    que convierte un CValue a un elemento Expr. Además, implemente las funciones
    cmplx+, cmplx- y cmplx0. Las primeras dos funciones suman y restan, respectivamente, dos valores del lenguaje. La última función retorna #t si el número
    complejo es 0.
    >>> (cmplx+ (compV 1 2) (compV 3 4))
    (compV 4 6)
    >>> (cmplx- (compV 1 2) (compV 3 4))
    (compV -2 -2)
    >>> (cmplx0? (compV 0 1))
    #f

(d) [10 Pt] Implemente la función subst que realiza la substitución de una expresión
    por un identificador. Note que una expresión with introduce identificadores que
    pueden ser utilizados en próximas definiciones, permitiendo que expresiones como
    la siguiente sean válidas:
    (with [ (x 2) (y (+ x 1))] (+ x y))
    La definición de y ocupa x.
    Su función de substitución debe tomar esto en cuenta y evitar substituir variables
    que han sido "oscurecidas" por otra definición local. Por ejemplo:
    ;; Este ejemplo no tiene "shadowing"
    >>> (subst (parser '(with [ (x 2) (y z) ] (+ x z))) 'z ( real 1))
    (withc (list (cons 'x ( real 2)) (cons 'y ( real 1))) (addc (idc 'x) ( real 1)))
    ;; En este ejemplo si ocurre "shadowing" -> no se realiza la substitucion
    >>> (subst (parser '(with [ (x 2) (y x)] (+ x x))) 'x ( real 1))
    (withc (list (cons 'x ( real 2)) (cons 'y (idc 'x))) (addc (idc 'x) (idc 'x)))
    Hint: Le puede ser útil definir una función auxiliar que realice una substitución
    sobre una lista de definiciones.

(e) [10 Pt] Implemente la función interp que reduce una expresión (Expr) en un
    valor del lenguaje (CValue). Si se encuentra una variable libre, arroje un error
    con el mensaje: "Free occurrence of a variable".

;; env-lookup :: symbol Env -> (or #f Env)

(define (env-lookup x env)

    [(xtEnv s v tail) (if (symbol=? x s)

**Exercise 1: Polynomial Language**
- (a) Define `Poly` datatype
- (b) Implement `parse` function
- (c) Implement `reduce` function

**Exercise 2: Complex Numbers Language**

- (b) Implement `parser`

Let me think about each part carefully.

**Exercise 1**

The `Poly` type from the BNF:
- (poly (Listof number))
- (id symbol)
- (add poly poly)
- (mul poly poly)
- (if0 poly poly poly)
- (with symbol poly poly)

So:
```racket
(deftype Poly
  (poly coeffs)
  (id s)
  (add l r)
  (mul l r)
  (if0 c t f)
  (with x e b))
```

**Parsing**:
- `0` -> `(poly '(0))` - special case: if 0, single number -> (poly '(0))
- `(3 1 0 0)` -> `(poly '(3 1))` - trailing zeros stripped
- `x` -> `(id 'x)`
- `(+ (3 5 7) (1 2))` -> `(add (poly '(3 5 7)) (poly '(1 2)))`
- `(* (2 1) (1 2))` -> `(mul (poly '(2 1)) (poly '(1 2)))`
- `(if0 (+ (3 5) (-3 2)) 1 0)` -> ...
- `(with x (1 2) (* 2 x))` -> `(with 'x (poly '(1 2)) (mul (poly '(2)) (id 'x)))`

So the rules:
- A number (atomic) is treated as `(poly (list n))` after trimming trailing zeros
- A symbol is `(id s)`
- A list of numbers is `(poly (list n...))` after trimming trailing zeros
- `(op ...)` recursively parse

Let me look at:
- `0` -> `(poly '(0))` - because the trim leaves just `(0)`
- `(3 1 0 0)` -> `(poly '(3 1))` - trailing zeros trimmed
- `(-3 2)` -> `(poly '(-3 2))`

What about a single number? `(3 5)` would be `(poly '(3 5))`, and `(3 0 0)` would be `(poly '(3))`.

What if the input is just a single negative number? Looking at the examples, the only single number used is `0` and `1`. Let me think...

For `0`, parsed as `(poly '(0))`. So when parsing a number, wrap it in `(list n)` and trim trailing zeros.

Actually wait, what if someone writes `parse -3`? It would be `(poly '(-3))`. That should be fine.

What if someone writes `parse '(0)`? An empty list of numbers? Probably doesn't make sense, ignore for now.

**Reduce**:
- `(poly coeffs)` -> just return it (already a poly)
- `(id x)` -> lookup in env
- `(add p1 p2)` -> add polys
- `(mul p1 p2)` -> multiply polys
- `(if0 c t f)` -> if c reduces to zero poly, return t, else return f
- `(with x e b)` -> extend env with (reduce e env), then reduce b in new env

Let me think about addition and multiplication of polynomials:
- A polynomial is a list of coefficients, where index i is the coefficient of x^i.
- Trim trailing zeros first (or normalize).
- Addition: align the two lists (the longer one wins), then sum coefficient by coefficient.
- Multiplication: standard polynomial multiplication.

```racket
;; trim-zeros: removes trailing zeros from a coefficient list
(define (trim-zeros lst)
  (let loop ([l lst])
    (cond
      [(null? l) '()]
      [(and (null? (cdr l)) (zero? (car l))) '()]
      [(zero? (last l)) (loop (reverse (cdr (reverse l))))]
      [else l])))
```

Actually a simpler approach:
```racket
(define (trim-zeros lst)
  (let ([r (reverse lst)])
    (cond
      [(null? r) '()]
      [(zero? (car r)) (trim-zeros (reverse (cdr r)))]
      [else lst])))
```

For addition:
```racket
(define (poly-add a b)
  (let ([a (trim-zeros a)]
        [b (trim-zeros b)])
    (let ([len (max (length a) (length b))])
      (build-list len
        (lambda (i)
          (+ (list-ref-safe a i 0) (list-ref-safe b i 0)))))))
```

Actually let me use a different approach. Let me make a helper that pads and adds:

```racket
(define (pad lst n)
  (append lst (build-list (- n (length lst)) (lambda (_) 0))))

(define (poly-add a b)
  (let ([len (max (length a) (length b))])
    (trim-zeros
      (map + (pad a len) (pad b len)))))
```

```racket
(define (poly-mul a b)

```

```racket
(define (poly-mul a b)
  (let* ([result-len (+ (length a) (length b) -1)]
         [result (build-list result-len (lambda (_) 0))])
    (for ([i (in-range (length a))])
      (for ([j (in-range (length b))])
        (let ([idx (+ i j)])
          (list-set! result idx 
                     (+ (list-ref result idx) 
                        (* (list-ref a i) (list-ref b j)))))))
    (trim-zeros result)))
```

But `play` doesn't have `for` loops. Let me use recursion:

```racket
(define (poly-mul a b)
  (define na (length a))
  (define nb (length b))
  (define (helper i result)
    (cond
      [(= i na) (trim-zeros result)]
      [else

           [(= j nb) (helper (+ i 1) result)]
           [else
            (define idx (+ i j))
            (list-set result idx
                      (+ (list-ref result idx)
                         (* (list-ref a i) (list-ref b j))))
            (inner (+ j 1) result)]))

  (helper 0 (build-list (+ na nb -1) (lambda (_) 0))))
```

Let me use a more straightforward style:

```racket
;; trim-zeros: list -> list
;; Removes trailing zeros from a polynomial coefficient list.
(define (trim-zeros lst)
  (cond
    [(null? lst) '()]
    [(null? (cdr lst)) 
     (if (zero? (car lst)) '() lst)]
    [else
     (let ([r (reverse lst)])
       (if (zero? (car r))
           (trim-zeros (reverse (cdr r)))
           lst))]))

;; normalize-poly: Poly -> Poly
;; Returns a poly in normalized form (with trimmed trailing zeros).
(define (normalize-poly p)
  (cond
    [(null? p) '()]
    [(zero? (car p)) (normalize-poly (cdr p))]
    [else p]))

;; poly-add: list list -> list
(define (poly-add a b)
  (let ([la (length a)] [lb (length b)])
    (let ([len (max la lb)])
      (normalize-poly
       (build-list len
         (lambda (i)
           (+ (if (< i la) (list-ref a i) 0)
              (if (< i lb) (list-ref b i) 0))))))))

;; poly-mul: list list -> list
(define (poly-mul a b)
  (let ([la (length a)] [lb (length b)])
    (let ([n (+ la lb -1)])
      (let ([result (build-list n (lambda (_) 0))])
        (let loop-i ([i 0])
          (cond
            [(= i la) (normalize-poly result)]
            [else
             (let loop-j ([j 0])
               (cond
                 [(= j lb) (loop-i (+ i 1))]
                 [else
                  (let ([idx (+ i j)])
                    (list-set result idx
                              (+ (list-ref result idx)
                                 (* (list-ref a i) (list-ref b j)))))
                  (loop-j (+ j 1))]))]))))))
```

```racket
;; nth: list n [default] -> value
;; Gets the nth element of a list, or default if out of bounds.
(define (nth lst n default)
  (cond
    [(null? lst) default]
    [(zero? n) (car lst)]
    [else (nth (cdr lst) (- n 1) default)]))

;; set-nth: list n value -> list
;; Sets the nth element of a list. Assumes n is in bounds.
(define (set-nth lst n v)
  (cond
    [(null? lst) '()]
    [(zero? n) (cons v (cdr lst))]
    [else (cons (car lst) (set-nth (cdr lst) (- n 1) v))]))

;; build-list-n: n proc -> list
(define (build-list-n n proc)
  (cond
    [(zero? n) '()]
    [else (cons (proc 0) (map-shift (proc) 1 (- n 1)))]))
```

OK so:
- `normalize-poly` works as expected
- `max`, `length`, `reverse`, `map` all work
- `map + lst1 lst2` works for adding two lists element-wise

Now let me think about Exercise 2.

**Exercise 2:**

Looking at the test cases:
- `parser '1` -> `(real 1)`
- `parser '(1 i)` -> `(imaginary 1)`
- `parser '(+ 2 (1 i))` -> `(addc (real 2) (imaginary 1))`

So we have:
- `real` and `imaginary` constructors
- For numeric literals: `1` becomes `(real 1)`, `(1 i)` becomes `(imaginary 1)`

Wait, but how does the parser distinguish a number from a list starting with a number? Let me think...

`parser '1` - input is a number 1, becomes `(real 1)`.
`parser '(1 i)` - input is a list `(1 i)`, becomes `(imaginary 1)`.
`parser '(+ 2 (1 i))` - input is `(+ 2 (1 i))`, becomes `(addc (real 2) (imaginary 1))`.

So:
- If the input is a number, return `(real n)`.
- If the input is a symbol, return `(idc s)`.
- If the input is `(n i)` for a number n, return `(imaginary n)`.
- If the input is `(+ e1 e2)`, return `(addc (parser e1) (parser e2))`.
- If the input is `(- e1 e2)`, return `(subc ...)`.

Now implementing:
```racket
(define (subst in what for)
  (match in
    [(real n) (real n)]
    [(imaginary n) (imaginary n)]
    [(idc s) (if (symbol=? s what) for (idc s))]
    [(addc l r) (addc (subst l what for) (subst r what for))]
    [(subc l r) (subc (subst l what for) (subst r what for))]
    [(if0c c t f) (if0c (subst c what for) (subst t what for) (subst f what for))]
    [(withc defs body)
     (withc (subst-defs defs what for)
            (subst-body body defs what for))]))

(define (subst-defs defs what for)
  ;; Substitute in each def, but stop substituting once a def shadows 'what'
  ;; Hmm but we should substitute for all defs where the variable doesn't shadow.
  (cond
    [(null? defs) '()]
    [else
     (let ([def (car defs)])
       (let ([var (car def)] [val (cdr def)])
         (cond
           [(symbol=? var what)
            ;; This def shadows 'what', so val is not substituted
            (cons (cons var val) 
                  ;; subsequent defs and body are also shadowed
                  (map (lambda (d) (cons (car d) (cdr d))) (cdr defs)))]
           [else
            (cons (cons var (subst val what for))
                  (subst-defs (cdr defs) what for))])))]))
```

Great. Now let me code this up:

```racket
;; subst :: Expr Symbol Expr -> Expr
(define (subst in what for)
  (match in
    [(real n) (real n)]
    [(imaginary n) (imaginary n)]
    [(idc s) (if (symbol=? s what) for (idc s))]
    [(addc l r) (addc (subst l what for) (subst r what for))]
    [(subc l r) (subc (subst l what for) (subst r what for))]
    [(if0c c t f) (if0c (subst c what for) (subst t what for) (subst f what for))]
    [(withc defs body) (subst-with in defs body what for)]))

;; subst-with: handles substitution in with, avoiding shadowing.
(define (subst-with original defs body what for)
  (let ([result (subst-defs defs what for)])
    (match result
      [(cons new-defs shadowed?)
       (withc new-defs (if shadowed? body (subst body what for)))])))

;; subst-defs: list -> (cons (listof (cons symbol Expr)) Boolean)
;; Returns the new list of defs and whether 'what' was shadowed.
(define (subst-defs defs what for)
  (subst-defs-helper defs what for #f '()))

(define (subst-defs-helper defs what for shadowed? acc)
  (cond
    [(null? defs) (cons (reverse acc) shadowed?)]
    [else
     (let ([def (car defs)])
       (let ([var (car def)] [val (cdr def)])
         (cond
           [shadowed?
            (subst-defs-helper (cdr defs) what for #t (cons def acc))]
           [(symbol=? var what)
            (subst-defs-helper (cdr defs) what for #t (cons def acc))]
           [else
            (subst-defs-helper (cdr defs) what for #f 
                               (cons (cons var (subst val what for)) acc))])))]))
```

```racket
(define (parser s-expr)
  (cond
    [(number? s-expr) (real s-expr)]
    [(symbol? s-expr) (idc s-expr)]
    [(pair? s-expr)
     (let ([head (car s-expr)])
       (cond
         [(eq? head '+) (addc (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr)))]
         [(eq? head '-) (subc (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr)))]
         [(eq? head 'if0) (if0c (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr)) (parser (cadddr s-expr)))]
         [(eq? head 'with)
          (let* ([defs (cadr s-expr)]
                 [body (caddr s-expr)])
            (cond
              [(null? defs) 
               (error 'parser "*with* expects at least one definition")]
              [else
               (withc (map parse-def defs) (parser body))]))]
         [(and (number? head) (eq? (cadr s-expr) 'i))
          (imaginary head)]
         [else (error 'parser "Invalid expression" s-expr)]))]
    [else (error 'parser "Invalid expression" s-expr)]))

(define (parse-def def)
  (cons (car def) (parser (cadr def))))
```

OK let me write the env-based interp:

```racket
;; Env: symbol -> CValue
(deftype Env
  (mtEnv)
  (xtEnv symbol CValue Env))

(define empty-env (mtEnv))

(define (extend-env x v env)
  (xtEnv x v env))

(define (env-lookup x env)
  (match env
    [(mtEnv) #f]
    [(xtEnv s v tail) (if (symbol=? x s) v (env-lookup x tail))]))

(define (interp expr)
  (interp-helper expr empty-env))

(define (interp-helper expr env)
  (match expr
    [(real n) (compV n 0)]
    [(imaginary n) (compV 0 n)]
    [(idc s)
     (let ([v (env-lookup s env)])
       (if v v (error 'interp "Free occurrence of a variable")))]
    [(addc l r) (cmplx+ (interp-helper l env) (interp-helper r env))]
    [(subc l r) (cmplx- (interp-helper l env) (interp-helper r env))]
    [(if0c c t f)
     (if (cmplx0? (interp-helper c env))
         (interp-helper t env)
         (interp-helper f env))]
    [(withc defs body)
     (interp-with defs body env)]))

(define (interp-with defs body env)
  (cond
    [(null? defs) (interp-helper body env)]
    [else
     (let ([def (car defs)])
       (let ([var (car def)] [val (cdr def)])
         (interp-with (cdr defs) body 
                      (extend-env var (interp-helper val env) env))))]))
```

```racket
;; reduce :: Poly Env -> Poly
(define (reduce p env)
  (match p
    [(poly coeffs) (poly (normalize-poly coeffs))]
    [(id s)
     (let ([v (env-lookup s env)])
       (if v v (error 'reduce "variable ~a is not defined" s)))]
    [(add l r) (poly-add-poly (reduce l env) (reduce r env))]
    [(mul l r) (poly-mul-poly (reduce l env) (reduce r env))]
    [(if0 c t f)
     (if (poly-zero? (reduce c env))
         (reduce t env)
         (reduce f env))]
    [(with x e b)
     (reduce b (extend-env x (reduce e env) env))]))

(define (poly-zero? p)
  (match p
    [(poly coeffs) (null? (normalize-poly coeffs))]
    [else (error 'poly-zero? "expected poly" p)]))

(define (poly-add-poly p1 p2)
  (match* (p1 p2)
    [((poly a) (poly b)) (poly (poly-add a b))]))

(define (poly-mul-poly p1 p2)
  (match* (p1 p2)
    [((poly a) (poly b)) (poly (poly-mul a b))]))
```

OK let me think about `poly-add` and `poly-mul` on lists:

```racket
;; normalize-poly: trims trailing zeros
(define (normalize-poly lst)
  (cond
    [(null? lst) '()]
    [(zero? (car lst)) (normalize-poly (cdr lst))]
    [else lst]))

;; poly-add: adds two coefficient lists
(define (poly-add a b)
  (let ([la (length a)] [lb (length b)])
    (let ([len (max la lb)])
      (normalize-poly
       (build-list len 
         (lambda (i)
           (+ (if (< i la) (list-ref a i) 0)
              (if (< i lb) (list-ref b i) 0))))))))

;; poly-mul: multiplies two coefficient lists
(define (poly-mul a b)
  (let ([la (length a)] [lb (length b)])
    (let ([n (+ la lb -1)])
      (let ([result (build-list n (lambda (_) 0))])
        (let loop-i ([i 0])
          (cond
            [(= i la) (normalize-poly result)]
            [else
             (let loop-j ([j 0])
               (cond
                 [(= j lb) (loop-i (+ i 1))]
                 [else
                  (let ([idx (+ i j)])
                    (list-set result idx
                              (+ (list-ref result idx)
                                 (* (list-ref a i) (list-ref b j)))))
                  (loop-j (+ j 1))]))]))))))
```

```racket
#lang play


#|
Nombre: Tarea 2
¿Utilizó Whiteboard Policy? (NO):

;;------------ ;;
;;==== P1 ==== ;;
;;------------ ;;

#| Parte A |#

#|
<poly> ::= (poly (Listof <number>))
         | (id <id>)
         | (add <poly> <poly>)
         | (mul <poly> <poly>)
         | (if0 <poly> <poly> <poly>)
         | (with <id> <poly> <poly>)
|#

(deftype Poly
  (poly coeffs)
  (id s)
  (add l r)
  (mul l r)
  (if0 c t f)
  (with x e b))

#| Parte B |#

#|
<s-poly> ::= <number>
           | <symbol>
           | (listof <number>)
           | (list + <s-poly> <s-poly> ...)
           | (list * <s-poly> <s-poly> ...)
           | (list if0 <s-poly> <s-poly> <s-poly>)
           | (list with <symbol> <s-poly> <s-poly>)
|#

;; parse :: <s-poly> -> Poly
;; Convierte una expresión en sintaxis concreta a la representación
;; abstracta Poly. Un número se representa como (poly (list n)) 
;; (normalizando ceros sobrantes).
(define (parse s)
  (cond
    [(number? s) (poly (normalize-coeffs (list s)))]
    [(symbol? s) (id s)]
    [(pair? s)
     (let ([head (car s)])
       (cond
         [(number? head) (poly (normalize-coeffs s))]
         [(eq? head '+) (add (parse (cadr s)) (parse (caddr s)))]
         [(eq? head '*) (mul (parse (cadr s)) (parse (caddr s)))]
         [(eq? head 'if0) 
          (if0 (parse (cadr s)) (parse (caddr s)) (parse (cadddr s)))]
         [(eq? head 'with) 
          (with (cadr s) (parse (caddr s)) (parse (cadddr s)))]
         [else (error 'parse "Invalid expression: ~a" s)]))]
    [else (error 'parse "Invalid expression: ~a" s)]))

#| Parte C |#

#|
<env> ::= (tyEnv)
         | (xtEnv <symbol> <poly> <env>)
|#

(deftype Env
  (tyEnv)
  (xtEnv symbol value env))

(define empty-env (tyEnv))

;; extend-env :: symbol Poly Env -> Env
;; Extiende un ambiente añadiendo una variable.
(define (extend-env x v env)
  (xtEnv x v env))

;; env-lookup :: symbol Env -> (or #f Poly)
;; Busca una variable en un ambiente, retornando el
;; valor encontrado o #f en caso de que no esté definida.
(define (env-lookup x env)
  (match env
    [(tyEnv) #f]
    [(xtEnv s v tail) (if (symbol=? x s)
                          v
                          (env-lookup x tail))]))

;; normalize-coeffs: list -> list
;; Elimina los ceros al final de la lista de coeficientes.
;; Si la lista queda vacía o sólo tiene ceros, devuelve (0).
(define (normalize-coeffs coeffs)
  (cond
    [(null? coeffs) '(0)]
    [(and (null? (cdr coeffs)) (zero? (car coeffs))) '(0)]
    [else
     (let loop ([rev (reverse coeffs)])
       (cond
         [(null? rev) '(0)]
         [(zero? (car rev)) (loop (cdr rev))]
         [else (reverse rev)]))]))

;; poly-coeffs: Poly -> list
;; Extrae los coeficientes de un Poly.
(define (poly-coeffs p)
  (match p
    [(poly c) c]
    [else (error 'poly-coeffs "expected poly, got ~a" p)]))

;; poly-zero?: Poly -> Boolean
;; Retorna #t si el polinomio es el polinomio cero.
(define (poly-zero? p)
  (match p
    [(poly c) 
     (cond
       [(null? c) #t]
       [(and (= 1 (length c)) (zero? (car c))) #t]
       [else #f])]
    [else #f]))

;; pad-zero: list num -> list
;; Rellena una lista con ceros hasta tener la longitud deseada.
(define (pad-zero lst n)
  (cond
    [(>= (length lst) n) lst]
    [else (append lst (build-list (- n (length lst)) (lambda (_) 0)))]))

;; poly-add-coeffs: list list -> list
;; Suma dos listas de coeficientes.
(define (poly-add-coeffs a b)
  (let ([len (max (length a) (length b))])
    (normalize-coeffs
     (let loop ([i 0] [a a] [b b] [acc '()])
       (cond
         [(= i len) (reverse acc)]
         [else
          (let ([av (if (null? a) 0 (car a))]
                [bv (if (null? b) 0 (car b))])
            (loop (+ i 1) 
                  (if (null? a) a (cdr a))
                  (if (null? b) b (cdr b))
                  (cons (+ av bv) acc)))])))))

;; poly-mul-coeffs: list list -> list
;; Multiplica dos listas de coeficientes (convolución).
(define (poly-mul-coeffs a b)
  (let ([la (length a)] [lb (length b)])
    (cond
      [(or (zero? la) (zero? lb)) '(0)]
      [else
       (let ([result (build-list (+ la lb -1) (lambda (_) 0))])
         (let loop-i ([i 0])
           (cond
             [(= i la) (normalize-coeffs result)]
             [else
              (let loop-j ([j 0])
                (cond
                  [(= j lb) (loop-i (+ i 1))]
                  [else
                   (list-set result (+ i j)
                             (+ (list-ref result (+ i j))
                                (* (list-ref a i) (list-ref b j))))
                   (loop-j (+ j 1))]))])))])))

;; reduce :: Poly Env -> Poly
;; Reduce un Poly a un polinomio puro (sin operaciones).
(define (reduce p env)
  (match p
    [(poly c) (poly (normalize-coeffs c))]
    [(id s)
     (let ([v (env-lookup s env)])
       (if v v (error 'reduce "variable ~a is not defined" s)))]
    [(add l r)
     (let ([p1 (reduce l env)] [p2 (reduce r env)])
       (poly (poly-add-coeffs (poly-coeffs p1) (poly-coeffs p2))))]
    [(mul l r)
     (let ([p1 (reduce l env)] [p2 (reduce r env)])
       (poly (poly-mul-coeffs (poly-coeffs p1) (poly-coeffs p2))))]
    [(if0 c t f)
     (if (poly-zero? (reduce c env))
         (reduce t env)
         (reduce f env))]
    [(with x e b)
     (reduce b (extend-env x (reduce e env) env))]))


;;------------ ;;
;;==== P2 ==== ;;
;;------------ ;;

#| Parte A |#

#|
<expr> ::= (real <number>)
         | (imaginary <number>)
         | (idc <symbol>)
         | (addc <expr> <expr>)
         | (subc <expr> <expr>)
         | (mulc <expr> <expr>)
         | (if0c <expr> <expr> <expr>)
         | (withc (listof (cons <symbol> <expr>)) <expr>)
|#

(deftype Expr
  (real n)
  (imaginary n)
  (idc s)
  (addc l r)
  (subc l r)
  (mulc l r)
  (if0c c t f)
  (withc defs body))

#| Parte B |#

#|
<s-expr> ::= <number>
           | <symbol>
           | (list <number> i)
           | (list + <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (list - <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (list * <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (list if0 <s-expr> <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (list with (listof (list <symbol> <s-expr>)) <s-expr>)
|#

;; parser :: <s-expr> -> Expr
;; Convierte una expresión en sintaxis concreta a la representación abstracta.
;; Lanza un error si with no recibe definiciones.
(define (parser s-expr)
  (cond
    [(number? s-expr) (real s-expr)]
    [(symbol? s-expr) (idc s-expr)]
    [(pair? s-expr)
     (let ([head (car s-expr)])
       (cond
         [(and (number? head) (eq? (cadr s-expr) 'i))
          (imaginary head)]
         [(eq? head '+) (addc (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr)))]
         [(eq? head '-) (subc (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr)))]
         [(eq? head '*) (mulc (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr)))]
         [(eq? head 'if0) 
          (if0c (parser (cadr s-expr)) 
                (parser (caddr s-expr)) 
                (parser (cadddr s-expr)))]
         [(eq? head 'with)
          (let* ([defs (cadr s-expr)]
                 [body (caddr s-expr)])
            (cond
              [(null? defs) 
               (error 'parser "*with* expects at least one definition")]
              [else
               (withc (map parse-def defs) (parser body))]))]
         [else (error 'parser "Invalid expression: ~a" s-expr)]))]
    [else (error 'parser "Invalid expression: ~a" s-expr)]))

;; parse-def: (list symbol s-expr) -> (cons symbol Expr)
;; Convierte una definición de with en un par (variable . expresión).
(define (parse-def def)
  (cons (car def) (parser (cadr def))))

#| Parte C |#

#|
<cvalue> ::= (compV <num> <num>)
|#

(deftype CValue (compV r i))

;; from-CValue :: CValue -> Expr
;; Convierte un CValue a un Expr.
(define (from-CValue v)
  (match v
    [(compV r i)
     (cond
       [(zero? i) (real r)]
       [else (imaginary i)])]))

;; cmplx+ :: CValue CValue -> CValue
;; Suma dos números complejos.
(define (cmplx+ v1 v2)
  (match* (v1 v2)
    [((compV r1 i1) (compV r2 i2))
     (compV (+ r1 r2) (+ i1 i2))]))

;; cmplx- :: CValue CValue -> CValue
;; Resta dos números complejos.
(define (cmplx- v1 v2)
  (match* (v1 v2)
    [((compV r1 i1) (compV r2 i2))
     (compV (- r1 r2) (- i1 i2))]))

;; cmplx0? :: CValue -> Boolean
;; Retorna #t si el número complejo es cero.
(define (cmplx0? v)
  (match v
    [(compV r i) (and (zero? r) (zero? i))]))

#| Parte D |#

;; subst :: Expr Symbol Expr -> Expr
;; Sustituye 'what' por 'for' en 'in', evitando variables
;; que han sido oscurecidas por una definición local.
(define (subst in what for)
  (match in
    [(real n) (real n)]
    [(imaginary n) (imaginary n)]
    [(idc s) (if (symbol=? s what) for (idc s))]
    [(addc l r) (addc (subst l what for) (subst r what for))]
    [(subc l r) (subc (subst l what for) (subst r what for))]
    [(mulc l r) (mulc (subst l what for) (subst r what for))]
    [(if0c c t f) 
     (if0c (subst c what for) (subst t what for) (subst f what for))]
    [(withc defs body)
     (let ([result (subst-defs defs what for)])
       (match result
         [(cons new-defs shadowed?)
          (withc new-defs 
                 (if shadowed? body (subst body what for)))]))]))

;; subst-defs: (listof (cons symbol Expr)) Symbol Expr 
;;           -> (cons (listof (cons symbol Expr)) Boolean)
;; Sustituye en una lista de definiciones. Retorna la nueva lista
;; y un boolean indicando si 'what' fue oscurecido por alguna definición.
(define (subst-defs defs what for)
  (let loop ([defs defs] [acc '()] [shadowed? #f])
    (cond
      [(null? defs) (cons (reverse acc) shadowed?)]
      [else
       (let ([def (car defs)])
         (let ([var (car def)] [val (cdr def)])
           (cond
             [shadowed?
              (loop (cdr defs) (cons def acc) #t)]
             [(symbol=? var what)
              (loop (cdr defs) (cons def acc) #t)]
             [else
              (loop (cdr defs) 
                    (cons (cons var (subst val what for)) acc)
                    #f)])))])))

#| Parte E |#

;; Env para interp: asocia identificadores con CValues
(deftype CEnv
  (mtEnv)
  (xtEnv symbol CValue CEnv))

(define empty-cenv (mtEnv))

;; extend-cenv :: symbol CValue CEnv -> CEnv
(define (extend-cenv x v env)
  (xtEnv x v env))

;; cenv-lookup :: symbol CEnv -> (or #f CValue)
(define (cenv-lookup x env)
  (match env
    [(mtEnv) #f]
    [(xtEnv s v tail) (if (symbol=? x s) v (cenv-lookup x tail))]))

;; interp :: Expr -> CValue
;; Reduce una expresión Expr a un CValue.
;; Arroja un error si hay una variable libre.
(define (interp expr)
  (interp-helper expr empty-cenv))

;; interp-helper :: Expr CEnv -> CValue
(define (interp-helper expr env)
  (match expr
    [(real n) (compV n 0)]
    [(imaginary n) (compV 0 n)]
    [(idc s)
     (let ([v (cenv-lookup s env)])
       (if v 
           v 
           (error 'interp "Free occurrence of a variable: ~a" s)))]
    [(addc l r) (cmplx+ (interp-helper l env) (interp-helper r env))]
    [(subc l r) (cmplx- (interp-helper l env) (interp-helper r env))]
    [(mulc l r) (cmplx* (interp-helper l env) (interp-helper r env))]
    [(if0c c t f)
     (if (cmplx0? (interp-helper c env))
         (interp-helper t env)
         (interp-helper f env))]
    [(withc defs body) (interp-with defs body env)]))

;; interp-with: procesa las definiciones de with y luego evalúa el cuerpo.
(define (interp-with defs body env)
  (cond
    [(null? defs) (interp-helper body env)]
    [else
     (let ([def (car defs)])
       (let ([var (car def)] [val (cdr def)])
         (interp-with (cdr defs) body 
                      (extend-cenv var (interp-helper val env) env))))]))
```

If a def has `var = what`, we set shadowed to `#t` and DON'T substitute in val.

```racket
(cond
  [shadowed?
   (loop (cdr defs) (cons def acc) #t)]
  [(symbol=? var what)
   (loop (cdr defs) (cons def acc) #t)]
  [else
   (loop (cdr defs) 
         (cons (cons var (subst val what for)) acc)
         #f)])
```

In the case `var = what`, we do `(cons def acc)`. `def` is the original def, not substituted. Good.

In the case `var != what` and not shadowed, we do `(cons (cons var (subst val what for)) acc)`. We substitute in val. Good.

In the case shadowed, we do `(cons def acc)`. We don't substitute. Good.

;; Polynomial multiplication using vectors
(define (normalize-coeffs coeffs)
  (cond
    ((null? coeffs) '(0))
    ((and (null? (cdr coeffs)) (zero? (car coeffs))) '(0))
    (else
     (let loop ((rev (reverse coeffs)))
       (cond
         ((null? rev) '(0))
         ((zero? (car rev)) (loop (cdr rev)))
         (else (reverse rev)))))))

(define (poly-mul-coeffs a b)
  (let ((la (length a)) (lb (length b)))
    (cond
      ((or (zero? la) (zero? lb)) '(0))
      (else
       (let ((result (make-vector (+ la lb -1) 0)))
         (let loop-i ((i 0))
           (cond
             ((= i la) (normalize-coeffs (vector->list result)))
             (else
              (let loop-j ((j 0))
                (cond
                  ((= j lb) (loop-i (+ i 1)))
                  (else
                   (vector-set! result (+ i j)
                                (+ (vector-ref result (+ i j))
                                   (* (list-ref a i) (list-ref b j))))
                   (loop-j (+ j 1)))))))))))))

(displayln (poly-mul-coeffs '(2 1) '(1 2)))
(displayln (poly-mul-coeffs '(1 1) '(1 1)))
(displayln (poly-mul-coeffs '(3) '(5)))

arguments.content:
#lang play


#|
Nombre: Tarea 2 CC4101
¿Utilizó Whiteboard Policy? (NO):
En caso afirmativo, ¿con quién?:
¿en qué ejercicio(s)?:
|#


;;------------ ;;
;;==== P1 ==== ;;
;;------------ ;;

#| Parte A |#

#|
<poly> ::= (poly (Listof <number>))
         | (id <id>)
         | (add <poly> <poly>)
         | (mul <poly> <poly>)
         | (if0 <poly> <poly> <poly>)
         | (with <id> <poly> <poly>)
|#

;; Poly es el tipo de dato recursivo que representa los AST de los
;; programas del lenguaje de polinomios.
(deftype Poly
  (poly coeffs)
  (id s)
  (add l r)
  (mul l r)
  (if0 c t f)
  (with x e b))

#| Parte B |#

#|
<s-poly> ::= <number>
           | <symbol>
           | (listof <number>)
           | (list + <s-poly> <s-poly> ...)
           | (list * <s-poly> <s-poly> ...)
           | (list if0 <s-poly> <s-poly> <s-poly>)
           | (list with <symbol> <s-poly> <s-poly>)
|#

;; normalize-coeffs : Listof(number) -> Listof(number)
;; Elimina los ceros sobrantes al final de una lista de coeficientes,
;; de manera que la lista resultante no tenga ceros finales, salvo
;; en el caso del polinomio cero, que se representa como (list 0).
(define (normalize-coeffs coeffs)
  (cond
    ((null? coeffs) '(0))
    ((and (null? (cdr coeffs)) (zero? (car coeffs))) '(0))
    (else
     (let loop ((rev (reverse coeffs)))
       (cond
         ((null? rev) '(0))
         ((zero? (car rev)) (loop (cdr rev)))
         (else (reverse rev)))))))

;; parse : <s-poly> -> Poly
;; Convierte una expresión en sintaxis concreta del lenguaje de
;; polinomios a su representación abstracta Poly.
(define (parse s)
  (cond
    ((number? s) (poly (normalize-coeffs (list s))))
    ((symbol? s) (id s))
    ((pair? s)
     (let ((head (car s)))
       (cond
         ((number? head) (poly (normalize-coeffs s)))
         ((eq? head '+) (add (parse (cadr s)) (parse (caddr s))))
         ((eq? head '*) (mul (parse (cadr s)) (parse (caddr s))))
         ((eq? head 'if0)
          (if0 (parse (cadr s)) (parse (caddr s)) (parse (cadddr s))))
         ((eq? head 'with)
          (with (cadr s) (parse (caddr s)) (parse (cadddr s))))
         (else (error 'parse "Expresión inválida: ~a" s)))))
    (else (error 'parse "Expresión inválida: ~a" s))))

#| Parte C |#

#|
<env> ::= (tyEnv)
         | (xtEnv <symbol> <poly> <env>)
|#

;; Env modela un ambiente donde las variables están asociadas a
;; polinomios puros (sin constructores add, mul, if0, with, id).
(deftype Env
  (tyEnv)
  (xtEnv symbol value env))

(define empty-env (tyEnv))

;; extend-env : symbol Poly Env -> Env
;; Extiende un ambiente añadiendo una asociación de variable a valor.
(define (extend-env x v env)
  (xtEnv x v env))

;; env-lookup : symbol Env -> (or #f Poly)
;; Busca una variable en un ambiente, retornando el valor asociado
;; o #f si la variable no se encuentra.
(define (env-lookup x env)
  (match env
    ((tyEnv) #f)
    ((xtEnv s v tail) (if (symbol=? x s) v (env-lookup x tail)))))

;; poly-coeffs : Poly -> Listof(number)
;; Extrae la lista de coeficientes de un polinomio puro.
;; Falla si el valor no es un polinomio puro.
(define (poly-coeffs p)
  (match p
    ((poly c) c)
    (else (error 'poly-coeffs "Se esperaba poly, se obtuvo: ~a" p))))

;; poly-zero? : Poly -> Boolean
;; Determina si un polinomio es el polinomio cero.
(define (poly-zero? p)
  (match p
    ((poly c)
     (cond
       ((null? c) #t)
       ((and (= 1 (length c)) (zero? (car c))) #t)
       (else #f)))
    (else #f)))

;; pad-list : Listof(number) number -> Listof(number)
;; Rellena una lista con ceros hasta alcanzar la longitud deseada.
(define (pad-list lst n)
  (cond
    ((>= (length lst) n) lst)
    (else (append lst (build-list (- n (length lst)) (lambda (_) 0))))))

;; poly-add-coeffs : Listof(number) Listof(number) -> Listof(number)
;; Suma dos listas de coeficientes y normaliza el resultado.
(define (poly-add-coeffs a b)
  (let ((len (max (length a) (length b))))
    (normalize-coeffs
     (let loop ((i 0) (a a) (b b) (acc '()))
       (cond
         ((= i len) (reverse acc))
         (else
          (let ((av (if (null? a) 0 (car a)))
                (bv (if (null? b) 0 (car b))))
            (loop (+ i 1)
                  (if (null? a) a (cdr a))
                  (if (null? b) b (cdr b))
                  (cons (+ av bv) acc)))))))))

;; poly-mul-coeffs : Listof(number) Listof(number) -> Listof(number)
;; Multiplica dos listas de coeficientes mediante convolución discreta.
;; Utiliza un vector mutable internamente para acumular los términos.
(define (poly-mul-coeffs a b)
  (let ((la (length a)) (lb (length b)))
    (cond
      ((or (zero? la) (zero? lb)) '(0))
      (else
       (let ((result (make-vector (+ la lb -1) 0)))
         (let loop-i ((i 0))
           (cond
             ((= i la) (normalize-coeffs (vector->list result)))
             (else
              (let loop-j ((j 0))
                (cond
                  ((= j lb) (loop-i (+ i 1)))
                  (else
                   (vector-set! result (+ i j)
                                (+ (vector-ref result (+ i j))
                                   (* (list-ref a i) (list-ref b j))))
                   (loop-j (+ j 1))))))))))))

;; reduce : Poly Env -> Poly
;; Reduce un programa Poly a un polinomio puro, evaluándolo en el
;; ambiente entregado.
(define (reduce p env)
  (match p
    ((poly c) (poly (normalize-coeffs c)))
    ((id s)
     (let ((v (env-lookup s env)))
       (if v v (error 'reduce "variable ~a is not defined" s))))
    ((add l r)
     (poly (poly-add-coeffs (poly-coeffs (reduce l env))
                            (poly-coeffs (reduce r env)))))
    ((mul l r)
     (poly (poly-mul-coeffs (poly-coeffs (reduce l env))
                            (poly-coeffs (reduce r env)))))
    ((if0 c t f)
     (if (poly-zero? (reduce c env))
         (reduce t env)
         (reduce f env)))
    ((with x e b)
     (reduce b (extend-env x (reduce e env) env)))))


;;------------ ;;
;;==== P2 ==== ;;
;;------------ ;;

#| Parte A |#

#|
<expr> ::= (real <number>)
         | (imaginary <number>)
         | (idc <symbol>)
         | (addc <expr> <expr>)
         | (subc <expr> <expr>)
         | (mulc <expr> <expr>)
         | (if0c <expr> <expr> <expr>)
         | (withc (Listof (cons <symbol> <expr>)) <expr>)
|#

;; Expr es el tipo de dato recursivo que representa los AST de un
;; lenguaje aritmético sobre los números complejos.
(deftype Expr
  (real n)
  (imaginary n)
  (idc s)
  (addc l r)
  (subc l r)
  (mulc l r)
  (if0c c t f)
  (withc defs body))

#| Parte B |#

#|
<s-expr> ::= <number>
           | <symbol>
           | (list <number> i)
           | (list + <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (list - <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (list * <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (list if0 <s-expr> <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (list with (listof (list <symbol> <s-expr>)) <s-expr>)
|#

;; parser : <s-expr> -> Expr
;; Convierte una expresión en sintaxis concreta del lenguaje de
;; números complejos a su representación abstracta Expr.
;; Lanza un error si una forma with no recibe definiciones.
(define (parser s-expr)
  (cond
    ((number? s-expr) (real s-expr))
    ((symbol? s-expr) (idc s-expr))
    ((pair? s-expr)
     (let ((head (car s-expr)))
       (cond
         ((and (number? head) (eq? (cadr s-expr) 'i))
          (imaginary head))
         ((eq? head '+) (addc (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr))))
         ((eq? head '-) (subc (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr))))
         ((eq? head '*) (mulc (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr))))
         ((eq? head 'if0)
          (if0c (parser (cadr s-expr))
                (parser (caddr s-expr))
                (parser (cadddr s-expr))))
         ((eq? head 'with)
          (let* ((defs (cadr s-expr))
                 (body (caddr s-expr)))
            (cond
              ((null? defs)
               (error 'parser "*with* expects at least one definition"))
              (else
               (withc (map parse-def defs) (parser body))))))
         (else (error 'parser "Expresión inválida: ~a" s-expr)))))
    (else (error 'parser "Expresión inválida: ~a" s-expr))))

;; parse-def : (list <symbol> <s-expr>) -> (cons <symbol> Expr)
;; Convierte una definición de un with a su representación abstracta.
(define (parse-def def)
  (cons (car def) (parser (cadr def))))

#| Parte C |#

#|
<cvalue> ::= (compV <num> <num>)
|#

;; CValue modela los valores del lenguaje: números complejos
;; representados como un par (parte-real . parte-imaginaria).
(deftype CValue (compV r i))

;; from-CValue : CValue -> Expr
;; Convierte un CValue a su equivalente como Expr atómico, devolviendo
;; un real si la parte imaginaria es nula y un imaginario en caso
;; contrario.
(define (from-CValue v)
  (match v
    ((compV r i)
     (cond
       ((zero? i) (real r))
       (else (imaginary i)))))

;; cmplx+ : CValue CValue -> CValue
;; Suma dos números complejos componente a componente.
(define (cmplx+ v1 v2)
  (match* (v1 v2)
    (((compV r1 i1) (compV r2 i2))
     (compV (+ r1 r2) (+ i1 i2)))))

;; cmplx- : CValue CValue -> CValue
;; Resta dos números complejos componente a componente.
(define (cmplx- v1 v2)
  (match* (v1 v2)
    (((compV r1 i1) (compV r2 i2))
     (compV (- r1 r2) (- i1 i2)))))

;; cmplx* : CValue CValue -> CValue

;; subst : Expr Symbol Expr -> Expr
;; Sustituye en la expresión 'in' toda ocurrencia libre del
;; identificador 'what' por la expresión 'for', teniendo en cuenta el
;; sombreo introducido por las definiciones locales.
(define (subst in what for)
  (match in
    ((real n) (real n))
    ((imaginary n) (imaginary n))
    ((idc s) (if (symbol=? s what) for (idc s)))
    ((addc l r) (addc (subst l what for) (subst r what for)))
    ((subc l r) (subc (subst l what for) (subst r what for)))
    ((mulc l r) (mulc (subst l what for) (subst r what for)))
    ((if0c c t f)
     (if0c (subst c what for) (subst t what for) (subst f what for)))
    ((withc defs body)
     (let ((result (subst-defs defs what for)))
       (match result
         ((cons new-defs shadowed?)
          (withc new-defs
                 (if shadowed? body (subst body what for))))))))

;; subst-defs : (Listof (cons symbol Expr)) Symbol Expr
;;           -> (cons (Listof (cons symbol Expr)) Boolean)
;; Recorre una lista de definiciones realizando sustitución. Cuando
;; una definición sombrea a 'what', no se sustituye en el resto de las
;; definiciones ni en el cuerpo. Devuelve la nueva lista de
;; definiciones y un indicador de si 'what' fue sombreado.
(define (subst-defs defs what for)
  (let loop ((defs defs) (acc '()) (shadowed? #f))
    (cond
      ((null? defs) (cons (reverse acc) shadowed?))
      (else
       (let ((def (car defs)))
         (let ((var (car def)) (val (cdr def)))
           (cond
             (shadowed?

             ((symbol=? var what)
              (loop (cdr defs) (cons def acc) #t))
             (else
              (loop (cdr defs)
                    (cons (cons var (subst val what for)) acc)
                    #f))))))))

;; CEnv modela un ambiente para interp donde las variables se
;; asocian a CValues.
(deftype CEnv
  (mtEnv)
  (xtEnv symbol value env))

(define empty-cenv (mtEnv))

;; extend-cenv : symbol CValue CEnv -> CEnv
;; Extiende un ambiente añadiendo una asociación de variable a valor.
(define (extend-cenv x v env)
  (xtEnv x v env))

;; cenv-lookup : symbol CEnv -> (or #f CValue)
;; Busca una variable en un ambiente, retornando el valor asociado
;; o #f si la variable no se encuentra.
(define (cenv-lookup x env)
  (match env
    ((mtEnv) #f)
    ((xtEnv s v tail) (if (symbol=? x s) v (cenv-lookup x tail)))))

;; interp : Expr -> CValue
;; Reduce una expresión Expr a un CValue. Si se encuentra una variable
;; libre, se arroja un error.
(define (interp expr)
  (interp-helper expr empty-cenv))

;; interp-helper : Expr CEnv -> CValue
;; Función auxiliar de interp que lleva el ambiente de evaluación.
(define (interp-helper expr env)
  (match expr
    ((real n) (compV n 0))
    ((imaginary n) (compV 0 n))
    ((idc s)
     (let ((v (cenv-lookup s env)))
       (if v v (error 'interp "Free occurrence of a variable: ~a" s))))

    ((subc l r) (cmplx- (interp-helper l env) (interp-helper r env)))
    ((mulc l r) (cmplx* (interp-helper l env) (interp-helper r env)))
    ((if0c c t f)
     (if (cmplx0? (interp-helper c env))
         (interp-helper t env)
         (interp-helper f env)))
    ((withc defs body) (interp-with defs body env))))

;; interp-with : (Listof (cons symbol Expr)) Expr CEnv -> CValue
;; Procesa secuencialmente las definiciones de un with, extendiendo
;; el ambiente con cada nuevo identificador antes de evaluar el cuerpo.
(define (interp-with defs body env)
  (cond
    ((null? defs) (interp-helper body env))
    (else
     (let ((def (car defs)))
       (let ((var (car def)) (val (cdr def)))
         (interp-with (cdr defs) body
                      (extend-cenv var (interp-helper val env) env)))))))

;; Expr es el tipo de dato recursivo que representa los AST de un
;; lenguaje aritmético sobre los números complejos.
(deftype Expr
  (real n)
  (imaginary n)
  (idc s)
  (addc l r)
  (subc l r)
  (mulc l r)
  (if0c c t f)
  (withc defs body))

#| Parte B |#

#|
<s-expr> ::= <number>
           | <symbol>
           | (list <number> i)
           | (list + <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (list - <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (list * <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (list if0 <s-expr> <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (list with (listof (list <symbol> <s-expr>)) <s-expr>)
|#

;; parser : <s-expr> -> Expr
;; Convierte una expresión en sintaxis concreta del lenguaje de
;; números complejos a su representación abstracta Expr.
;; Lanza un error si una forma with no recibe definiciones.
(define (parser s-expr)
  (cond
    ((number? s-expr) (real s-expr))
    ((symbol? s-expr) (idc s-expr))
    ((pair? s-expr)
     (let ((head (car s-expr)))
       (cond
         ((and (number? head) (eq? (cadr s-expr) 'i))
          (imaginary head))
         ((eq? head '+) (addc (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr))))
         ((eq? head '-) (subc (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr))))
         ((eq? head '*) (mulc (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr))))
         ((eq? head 'if0)
          (if0c (parser (cadr s-expr))
                (parser (caddr s-expr))
                (parser (cadddr s-expr))))
         ((eq? head 'with)
          (let* ((defs (cadr s-expr))
                 (body (caddr s-expr)))
            (cond
              ((null? defs)
               (error 'parser "*with* expects at least one definition"))
              (else
               (withc (map parse-def defs) (parser body))))))
         (else (error 'parser "Expresión inválida: ~a" s-expr)))))
    (else (error 'parser "Expresión inválida: ~a" s-expr))))

;; parse-def : (list <symbol> <s-expr>) -> (cons <symbol> Expr)
;; Convierte una definición de un with a su representación abstracta.
(define (parse-def def)
  (cons (car def) (parser (cadr def))))

;; CValue modela los valores del lenguaje: números complejos

;; from-CValue : CValue -> Expr
;; Convierte un CValue a su equivalente como Expr atómico, devolviendo
;; un real si la parte imaginaria es nula y un imaginario en caso
;; contrario.
(define (from-CValue v)
  (match v
    ((compV r i)
     (cond
       ((zero? i) (real r))
       (else (imaginary i)))))

#lang racket
;; Show depth at end of each line
(define (check-parens filename)
  (define in (open-input-file filename))
  (define chars (port->string in))
  (close-input-port in)
  (define N (string-length chars))
  (let loop ([i 0] [depth 0] [line 1])
    (cond
      [(= i N)
       (printf "End: depth=~a~n" depth)]
      [else
       (define c (string-ref chars i))
       (cond
         [(char=? c #\()
          (loop (+ i 1) (+ depth 1) line)]
         [(char=? c #\))
          (when (= depth 0)
            (printf "Unexpected close at line ~a~n" line))
          (loop (+ i 1) (- depth 1) line)]
         [(char=? c #\newline)
          (printf "Line ~a end: depth=~a~n" line depth)
          (loop (+ i 1) depth (+ line 1))]
         [else
          (loop (+ i 1) depth line)])])))
(check-parens "/workspace/T2.rkt")

| (list * <s-expr> <s-expr>)

;; parser : <s-expr> -> Expr
;; Convierte una expresión en sintaxis concreta del lenguaje de
;; números complejos a su representación abstracta Expr.
;; Lanza un error si una forma with no recibe definiciones.
(define (parser s-expr)
  (cond
    ((number? s-expr) (real s-expr))
    ((symbol? s-expr) (idc s-expr))
    ((pair? s-expr)
     (let ((head (car s-expr)))
       (cond
         ((and (number? head) (eq? (cadr s-expr) 'i))
          (imaginary head))
         ((eq? head '+) (addc (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr))))
         ((eq? head '-) (subc (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr))))
         ((eq? head '*) (mulc (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr))))
         ((eq? head 'if0)
          (if0c (parser (cadr s-expr))
                (parser (caddr s-expr))
                (parser (cadddr s-expr))))
         ((eq? head 'with)
          (let* ((defs (cadr s-expr))
                 (body (caddr s-expr)))
            (cond
              ((null? defs)
               (error 'parser "*with* expects at least one definition"))
              (else
               (withc (map parse-def defs) (parser body))))))
         (else (error 'parser "Expresión inválida: ~a" s-expr)))))
    (else (error 'parser "Expresión inválida: ~a" s-expr))))

;; parse-def : (list <symbol> <s-expr>) -> (cons <symbol> Expr)
;; Convierte una definición de un with a su representación abstracta.
(define (parse-def def)
  (cons (car def) (parser (cadr def))))

;; CValue modela los valores del lenguaje: números complejos
;; representados como un par (parte-real . parte-imaginaria).
(deftype CValue (compV r i))

;; from-CValue : CValue -> Expr
;; Convierte un CValue a su equivalente como Expr atómico, devolviendo
;; un real si la parte imaginaria es nula y un imaginario en caso
;; contrario.
(define (from-CValue v)
  (match v
    ((compV r i)
     (cond
       ((zero? i) (real r))
       (else (imaginary i)))))

;; cmplx+ : CValue CValue -> CValue
;; Suma dos números complejos componente a componente.
(define (cmplx+ v1 v2)
  (match* (v1 v2)
    (((compV r1 i1) (compV r2 i2))
     (compV (+ r1 r2) (+ i1 i2)))))

;; cmplx- : CValue CValue -> CValue
;; Resta dos números complejos componente a componente.
(define (cmplx- v1 v2)
  (match* (v1 v2)
    (((compV r1 i1) (compV r2 i2))
     (compV (- r1 r2) (- i1 i2)))))

;; cmplx* : CValue CValue -> CValue
;; Multiplica dos números complejos según (a+bi)(c+di) = (ac-bd) + (ad+bc)i.
(define (cmplx* v1 v2)
  (match* (v1 v2)
    (((compV r1 i1) (compV r2 i2))
     (compV (- (* r1 r2) (* i1 i2))
            (+ (* r1 i2) (* i1 r2))))))

;; cmplx0? : CValue -> Boolean
;; Determina si un número complejo es el cero.
(define (cmplx0? v)
  (match v
    ((compV r i) (and (zero? r) (zero? i)))))

#| Parte D |#

    ((real n) (real n))
    ((imaginary n) (imaginary n))

((idc s) (if (symbol=? s what) for (idc s)))
    ((addc l r) (addc (subst l what for) (subst r what for)))
    ((subc l r) (subc (subst l what for) (subst r what for)))
    ((mulc l r) (mulc (subst l what for) (subst r what for)))
    ((if0c c t f)
     (if0c (subst c what for) (subst t what for) (subst f what for)))
    ((withc defs body)
     (let ((result (subst-defs defs what for)))
       (match result
         ((cons new-defs shadowed?)
          (withc new-defs
                 (if shadowed? body (subst body what for))))))))

;; subst-defs : (Listof (cons symbol Expr)) Symbol Expr
;;           -> (cons (Listof (cons symbol Expr)) Boolean)
;; Recorre una lista de definiciones realizando sustitución. Cuando
;; una definición sombrea a 'what', no se sustituye en el resto de las
;; definiciones ni en el cuerpo. Devuelve la nueva lista de
;; definiciones y un indicador de si 'what' fue sombreado.
(define (subst-defs defs what for)
  (let loop ((defs defs) (acc '()) (shadowed? #f))
    (cond
      ((null? defs) (cons (reverse acc) shadowed?))
      (else
       (let ((def (car defs)))
         (let ((var (car def)) (val (cdr def)))
           (cond
             (shadowed?
              (loop (cdr defs) (cons def acc) #t))
             ((symbol=? var what)
              (loop (cdr defs) (cons def acc) #t))
             (else
              (loop (cdr defs)
                    (cons (cons var (subst val what for)) acc)
                    #f))))))))

#|
<cenv> ::= (mtEnv)
          | (xtEnv <symbol> <cvalue> <cenv>)
|#

;; CEnv modela un ambiente para interp donde las variables se
;; asocian a CValues.
(deftype CEnv
  (mtEnv)
  (xtEnv symbol value env))

(define empty-cenv (mtEnv))

;; extend-cenv : symbol CValue CEnv -> CEnv
;; Extiende un ambiente añadiendo una asociación de variable a valor.
(define (extend-cenv x v env)
  (xtEnv x v env))

;; cenv-lookup : symbol CEnv -> (or #f CValue)
;; Busca una variable en un ambiente, retornando el valor asociado
;; o #f si la variable no se encuentra.
(define (cenv-lookup x env)
  (match env
    ((mtEnv) #f)
    ((xtEnv s v tail) (if (symbol=? x s) v (cenv-lookup x tail)))))

;; interp : Expr -> CValue
;; Reduce una expresión Expr a un CValue. Si se encuentra una variable
;; libre, se arroja un error.
(define (interp expr)
  (interp-helper expr empty-cenv))

;; interp-helper : Expr CEnv -> CValue
;; Función auxiliar de interp que lleva el ambiente de evaluación.
(define (interp-helper expr env)
  (match expr
    ((real n) (compV n 0))
    ((imaginary n) (compV 0 n))
    ((idc s)
     (let ((v (cenv-lookup s env)))
       (if v v (error 'interp "Free occurrence of a variable: ~a" s))))
    ((addc l r) (cmplx+ (interp-helper l env) (interp-helper r env)))
    ((subc l r) (cmplx- (interp-helper l env) (interp-helper r env)))
    ((mulc l r) (cmplx* (interp-helper l env) (interp-helper r env)))
    ((if0c c t f)
     (if (cmplx0? (interp-helper c env))
         (interp-helper t env)
         (interp-helper f env)))
    ((withc defs body) (interp-with defs body env))))

;; interp-with : (Listof (cons symbol Expr)) Expr CEnv -> CValue
;; Procesa secuencialmente las definiciones de un with, extendiendo
;; el ambiente con cada nuevo identificador antes de evaluar el cuerpo.
(define (interp-with defs body env)
  (cond
    ((null? defs) (interp-helper body env))
    (else
     (let ((def (car defs)))
       (let ((var (car def)) (val (cdr def)))
         (interp-with (cdr defs) body
                      (extend-cenv var (interp-helper val env) env)))))))

         ((eq? head '-) (subc (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr))))
         ((eq? head '*) (mulc (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr))))
         ((eq? head 'if0)
          (if0c (parser (cadr s-expr))
                (parser (caddr s-expr))
                (parser (cadddr s-expr))))
         ((eq? head 'with)
          (let* ((defs (cadr s-expr))
                 (body (caddr s-expr)))
            (cond
              ((null? defs)
               (error 'parser "*with* expects at least one definition"))
              (else
               (withc (map parse-def defs) (parser body))))))
         (else (error 'parser "Expresión inválida: ~a" s-expr)))))
    (else (error 'parser "Expresión inválida: ~a" s-expr))))

(define (parse-def def)
  (cons (car def) (parser (cadr def))))

(displayln (parser 1))
(displayln (parser '(1 i)))
(displayln (parser '(+ 2 (1 i))))

;; Paren counter with line tracking
(define in (open-input-file "/workspace/T2.rkt"))
(define chars (port->string in))
(close-input-port in)
(define N (string-length chars))
(define result '())
(let loop ([i 0] [depth 0] [line 1] [max-depth 0])
  (cond
    [(= i N) result]
    [else
     (define c (string-ref chars i))
     (cond
       [(char=? c #\() (loop (+ i 1) (+ depth 1) line (max max-depth (+ depth 1)))]
       [(char=? c #\))
        (define new-depth (- depth 1))
        (define new-result
          (if (and (= new-depth 0) (not (= depth 0)))
              (cons (cons line depth) result)
              result))
        (loop (+ i 1) new-depth line max-depth)]
       [(char=? c #\newline) (loop (+ i 1) depth (+ line 1) max-depth)]
       [else (loop (+ i 1) depth line max-depth)])]))
;; Show only lines where depth changes from a higher value to 0

(for ([entry (reverse result)])

;; Utiliza un vector mutable internamente para acumular los términos.
(define (poly-mul-coeffs a b)
  (let ((la (length a)) (lb (length b)))
    (cond
      ((or (zero? la) (zero? lb)) '(0))
      (else
       (let ((result (make-vector (+ la lb -1) 0)))
         (let loop-i ((i 0))
           (cond
             ((= i la) (normalize-coeffs (vector->list result)))
             (else
              (let loop-j ((j 0))
                (cond
                  ((= j lb) (loop-i (+ i 1)))
                  (else
                   (vector-set! result (+ i j)
                                (+ (vector-ref result (+ i j))
                                   (* (list-ref a i) (list-ref b j))))
                   (loop-j (+ j 1))))))))))))

;; reduce : Poly Env -> Poly
;; Reduce un programa Poly a un polinomio puro, evaluándolo en el
;; ambiente entregado.
(define (reduce p env)
  (match p
    ((poly c) (poly (normalize-coeffs c)))
    ((id s)
     (let ((v (env-lookup s env)))
       (if v v (error 'reduce "variable ~a is not defined" s))))
    ((add l r)
     (poly (poly-add-coeffs (poly-coeffs (reduce l env))
                            (poly-coeffs (reduce r env)))))
    ((mul l r)
     (poly (poly-mul-coeffs (poly-coeffs (reduce l env))
                            (poly-coeffs (reduce r env)))))
    ((if0 c t f)
     (if (poly-zero? (reduce c env))
         (reduce t env)
         (reduce f env)))
    ((with x e b)
     (reduce b (extend-env x (reduce e env) env)))))

#lang play

#|
Nombre: Tarea 2 CC4101
¿Utilizó Whiteboard Policy? (NO):
En caso afirmativo, ¿con quién?:
¿en qué ejercicio(s)?:
|#

;;------------ ;;
;;==== P1 ==== ;;
;;------------ ;;

#| Parte A |#

#|
<poly> ::= (poly (Listof <number>))
         | (id <id>)
         | (add <poly> <poly>)
         | (mul <poly> <poly>)
         | (if0 <poly> <poly> <poly>)
         | (with <id> <poly> <poly>)
|#

;; Poly es el tipo de dato recursivo que representa los AST de los
;; programas del lenguaje de polinomios.
(deftype Poly
  (poly coeffs)
  (id s)
  (add l r)
  (mul l r)
  (if0 c t f)
  (with x e b))

#| Parte B |#

#|
<s-poly> ::= <number>
           | <symbol>
           | (listof <number>)
           | (list + <s-poly> <s-poly> ...)
           | (list * <s-poly> <s-poly> ...)
           | (list if0 <s-poly> <s-poly> <s-poly>)
           | (list with <symbol> <s-poly> <s-poly>)
|#

;; normalize-coeffs : Listof(number) -> Listof(number)
;; Elimina los ceros sobrantes al final de una lista de coeficientes,
;; de manera que la lista resultante no tenga ceros finales, salvo
;; en el caso del polinomio cero, que se representa como (list 0).
(define (normalize-coeffs coeffs)
  (cond
    ((null? coeffs) '(0))
    ((and (null? (cdr coeffs)) (zero? (car coeffs))) '(0))
    (else
     (let loop ((rev (reverse coeffs)))
       (cond
         ((null? rev) '(0))
         ((zero? (car rev)) (loop (cdr rev)))
         (else (reverse rev)))))))

;; parse : <s-poly> -> Poly
;; Convierte una expresión en sintaxis concreta del lenguaje de
;; polinomios a su representación abstracta Poly.
(define (parse s)
  (cond
    ((number? s) (poly (normalize-coeffs (list s))))
    ((symbol? s) (id s))
    ((pair? s)
     (let ((head (car s)))
       (cond
         ((number? head) (poly (normalize-coeffs s)))
         ((eq? head '+) (add (parse (cadr s)) (parse (caddr s))))
         ((eq? head '*) (mul (parse (cadr s)) (parse (caddr s))))
         ((eq? head 'if0)
          (if0 (parse (cadr s)) (parse (caddr s)) (parse (cadddr s))))
         ((eq? head 'with)
          (with (cadr s) (parse (caddr s)) (parse (cadddr s))))
         (else (error 'parse "Expresión inválida: ~a" s)))))
    (else (error 'parse "Expresión inválida: ~a" s))))

#| Parte C |#

#|
<env> ::= (tyEnv)
         | (xtEnv <symbol> <poly> <env>)
|#

;; Env modela un ambiente donde las variables están asociadas a
;; polinomios puros (sin constructores add, mul, if0, with, id).
(deftype Env
  (tyEnv)
  (xtEnv symbol value env))

(define empty-env (tyEnv))

;; extend-env : symbol Poly Env -> Env
;; Extiende un ambiente añadiendo una asociación de variable a valor.
(define (extend-env x v env)
  (xtEnv x v env))

;; env-lookup : symbol Env -> (or #f Poly)
;; Busca una variable en un ambiente, retornando el valor asociado
;; o #f si la variable no se encuentra.
(define (env-lookup x env)
  (match env
    ((tyEnv) #f)
    ((xtEnv s v tail) (if (symbol=? x s) v (env-lookup x tail)))))

;; poly-coeffs : Poly -> Listof(number)
;; Extrae la lista de coeficientes de un polinomio puro.
;; Falla si el valor no es un polinomio puro.
(define (poly-coeffs p)
  (match p
    ((poly c) c)
    (else (error 'poly-coeffs "Se esperaba poly, se obtuvo: ~a" p))))

;; poly-zero? : Poly -> Boolean
;; Determina si un polinomio es el polinomio cero.
(define (poly-zero? p)
  (match p
    ((poly c)
     (cond
       ((null? c) #t)
       ((and (= 1 (length c)) (zero? (car c))) #t)
       (else #f)))
    (else #f)))

;; poly-add-coeffs : Listof(number) Listof(number) -> Listof(number)
;; Suma dos listas de coeficientes y normaliza el resultado.
(define (poly-add-coeffs a b)
  (let ((len (max (length a) (length b))))
    (normalize-coeffs
     (let loop ((i 0) (a a) (b b) (acc '()))
       (cond
         ((= i len) (reverse acc))
         (else
          (let ((av (if (null? a) 0 (car a)))
                (bv (if (null? b) 0 (car b))))
            (loop (+ i 1)
                  (if (null? a) a (cdr a))
                  (if (null? b) b (cdr b))
                  (cons (+ av bv) acc)))))))))

;; poly-mul-coeffs : Listof(number) Listof(number) -> Listof(number)
;; Multiplica dos listas de coeficientes mediante convolución discreta.
;; Utiliza un vector mutable internamente para acumular los términos.
(define (poly-mul-coeffs a b)
  (let ((la (length a)) (lb (length b)))
    (cond
      ((or (zero? la) (zero? lb)) '(0))
      (else
       (let ((result (make-vector (+ la lb -1) 0)))
         (let loop-i ((i 0))
           (cond
             ((= i la) (normalize-coeffs (vector->list result)))
             (else
              (let loop-j ((j 0))
                (cond
                  ((= j lb) (loop-i (+ i 1)))
                  (else
                   (vector-set! result (+ i j)
                                (+ (vector-ref result (+ i j))
                                   (* (list-ref a i) (list-ref b j))))
                   (loop-j (+ j 1))))))))))))

;; reduce : Poly Env -> Poly
;; Reduce un programa Poly a un polinomio puro, evaluándolo en el
;; ambiente entregado.
(define (reduce p env)
  (match p
    ((poly c) (poly (normalize-coeffs c)))
    ((id s)
     (let ((v (env-lookup s env)))
       (if v v (error 'reduce "variable ~a is not defined" s))))
    ((add l r)
     (poly (poly-add-coeffs (poly-coeffs (reduce l env))
                            (poly-coeffs (reduce r env)))))
    ((mul l r)
     (poly (poly-mul-coeffs (poly-coeffs (reduce l env))
                            (poly-coeffs (reduce r env)))))
    ((if0 c t f)
     (if (poly-zero? (reduce c env))
         (reduce t env)
         (reduce f env)))
    ((with x e b)
     (reduce b (extend-env x (reduce e env) env)))))

;;------------ ;;
;;==== P2 ==== ;;
;;------------ ;;

#| Parte A |#

#|
<expr> ::= (real <number>)
         | (imaginary <number>)
         | (idc <symbol>)
         | (addc <expr> <expr>)
         | (subc <expr> <expr>)
         | (mulc <expr> <expr>)
         | (if0c <expr> <expr> <expr>)
         | (withc (Listof (cons <symbol> <expr>)) <expr>)
|#

;; Expr es el tipo de dato recursivo que representa los AST de un
;; lenguaje aritmético sobre los números complejos.
(deftype Expr
  (real n)
  (imaginary n)
  (idc s)
  (addc l r)
  (subc l r)
  (mulc l r)
  (if0c c t f)
  (withc defs body))

#| Parte B |#

#|
<s-expr> ::= <number>
           | <symbol>
           | (list <number> i)
           | (list + <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (list - <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (list * <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (list if0 <s-expr> <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (list with (listof (list <symbol> <s-expr>)) <s-expr>)
|#

;; parser : <s-expr> -> Expr
;; Convierte una expresión en sintaxis concreta del lenguaje de
;; números complejos a su representación abstracta Expr.
;; Lanza un error si una forma with no recibe definiciones.
(define (parser s-expr)
  (cond
    ((number? s-expr) (real s-expr))
    ((symbol? s-expr) (idc s-expr))
    ((pair? s-expr)
     (let ((head (car s-expr)))
       (cond
         ((and (number? head) (eq? (cadr s-expr) 'i))
          (imaginary head))
         ((eq? head '+) (addc (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr))))
         ((eq? head '-) (subc (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr))))
         ((eq? head '*) (mulc (parser (cadr s-expr)) (parser (caddr s-expr))))
         ((eq? head 'if0)
          (if0c (parser (cadr s-expr))
                (parser (caddr s-expr))
                (parser (cadddr s-expr))))
         ((eq? head 'with)
          (let* ((defs (cadr s-expr))
                 (body (caddr s-expr)))
            (cond
              ((null? defs)
               (error 'parser "*with* expects at least one definition"))
              (else
               (withc (map parse-def defs) (parser body))))))
         (else (error 'parser "Expresión inválida: ~a" s-expr)))))
    (else (error 'parser "Expresión inválida: ~a" s-expr))))

;; parse-def : (list <symbol> <s-expr>) -> (cons <symbol> Expr)
;; Convierte una definición de un with a su representación abstracta.
(define (parse-def def)
  (cons (car def) (parser (cadr def))))

#| Parte C |#

#|
<cvalue> ::= (compV <num> <num>)
|#

;; CValue modela los valores del lenguaje: números complejos
;; representados como un par (parte-real . parte-imaginaria).
(deftype CValue (compV r i))

;; from-CValue : CValue -> Expr
;; Convierte un CValue a su equivalente como Expr atómico, devolviendo
;; un real si la parte imaginaria es nula y un imaginario en caso
;; contrario.
(define (from-CValue v)
  (match v
    ((compV r i)
     (cond
       ((zero? i) (real r))
       (else (imaginary i))))))

;; cmplx+ : CValue CValue -> CValue
;; Suma dos números complejos componente a componente.
(define (cmplx+ v1 v2)
  (match* (v1 v2)
    (((compV r1 i1) (compV r2 i2))
     (compV (+ r1 r2) (+ i1 i2)))))

;; cmplx- : CValue CValue -> CValue
;; Resta dos números complejos componente a componente.
(define (cmplx- v1 v2)
  (match* (v1 v2)
    (((compV r1 i1) (compV r2 i2))
     (compV (- r1 r2) (- i1 i2)))))

;; cmplx* : CValue CValue -> CValue
;; Multiplica dos números complejos según (a+bi)(c+di) = (ac-bd) + (ad+bc)i.
(define (cmplx* v1 v2)
  (match* (v1 v2)
    (((compV r1 i1) (compV r2 i2))
     (compV (- (* r1 r2) (* i1 i2))
            (+ (* r1 i2) (* i1 r2))))))

;; cmplx0? : CValue -> Boolean
;; Determina si un número complejo es el cero.
(define (cmplx0? v)
  (match v
    ((compV r i) (and (zero? r) (zero? i)))))

#| Parte D |#

;; subst : Expr Symbol Expr -> Expr
;; Sustituye en la expresión 'in' toda ocurrencia libre del
;; identificador 'what' por la expresión 'for', teniendo en cuenta el
;; sombreo introducido por las definiciones locales.
(define (subst in what for)
  (match in
    ((real n) (real n))
    ((imaginary n) (imaginary n))
    ((idc s) (if (symbol=? s what) for (idc s)))
    ((addc l r) (addc (subst l what for) (subst r what for)))
    ((subc l r) (subc (subst l what for) (subst r what for)))
    ((mulc l r) (mulc (subst l what for) (subst r what for)))
    ((if0c c t f)
     (if0c (subst c what for) (subst t what for) (subst f what for)))
    ((withc defs body)
     (let ((result (subst-defs defs what for)))
       (match result
         ((cons new-defs shadowed?)
          (withc new-defs
                 (if shadowed? body (subst body what for)))))))))

;; subst-defs : (Listof (cons symbol Expr)) Symbol Expr
;;           -> (cons (Listof (cons symbol Expr)) Boolean)
;; Recorre una lista de definiciones realizando sustitución. Cuando
;; una definición sombrea a 'what', no se sustituye en el resto de las
;; definiciones ni en el cuerpo. Devuelve la nueva lista de
;; definiciones y un indicador de si 'what' fue sombreado.
(define (subst-defs defs what for)
  (let loop ((defs defs) (acc '()) (shadowed? #f))
    (cond
      ((null? defs) (cons (reverse acc) shadowed?))
      (else
       (let ((def (car defs)))
         (let ((var (car def)) (val (cdr def)))
           (cond
             (shadowed?
              (loop (cdr defs) (cons def acc) #t))
             ((symbol=? var what)
              (loop (cdr defs) (cons def acc) #t))
             (else
              (loop (cdr defs)
                    (cons (cons var (subst val what for)) acc)
                    #f)))))))))

#| Parte E |#

#|
<cenv> ::= (mtEnv)
          | (xtEnv <symbol> <cvalue> <cenv>)
|#

;; CEnv modela un ambiente para interp donde las variables se
;; asocian a CValues.
(deftype CEnv
  (mtEnv)
  (xtEnv symbol value env))

(define empty-cenv (mtEnv))

;; extend-cenv : symbol CValue CEnv -> CEnv
;; Extiende un ambiente añadiendo una asociación de variable a valor.
(define (extend-cenv x v env)
  (xtEnv x v env))

;; cenv-lookup : symbol CEnv -> (or #f CValue)
;; Busca una variable en un ambiente, retornando el valor asociado
;; o #f si la variable no se encuentra.
(define (cenv-lookup x env)
  (match env
    ((mtEnv) #f)
    ((xtEnv s v tail) (if (symbol=? x s) v (cenv-lookup x tail)))))

;; interp : Expr -> CValue
;; Reduce una expresión Expr a un CValue. Si se encuentra una variable
;; libre, se arroja un error.
(define (interp expr)
  (interp-helper expr empty-cenv))

;; interp-helper : Expr CEnv -> CValue
;; Función auxiliar de interp que lleva el ambiente de evaluación.
(define (interp-helper expr env)
  (match expr
    ((real n) (compV n 0))
    ((imaginary n) (compV 0 n))
    ((idc s)
     (let ((v (cenv-lookup s env)))
       (if v v (error 'interp "Free occurrence of a variable: ~a" s))))
    ((addc l r) (cmplx+ (interp-helper l env) (interp-helper r env)))
    ((subc l r) (cmplx- (interp-helper l env) (interp-helper r env)))
    ((mulc l r) (cmplx* (interp-helper l env) (interp-helper r env)))
    ((if0c c t f)
     (if (cmplx0? (interp-helper c env))
         (interp-helper t env)
         (interp-helper f env)))
    ((withc defs body) (interp-with defs body env))))

;; interp-with : (Listof (cons symbol Expr)) Expr CEnv -> CValue
;; Procesa secuencialmente las definiciones de un with, extendiendo
;; el ambiente con cada nuevo identificador antes de evaluar el cuerpo.
(define (interp-with defs body env)
  (cond
    ((null? defs) (interp-helper body env))
    (else
     (let ((def (car defs)))
       (let ((var (car def)) (val (cdr def)))
         (interp-with (cdr defs) body
                      (extend-cenv var (interp-helper val env) env)))))))

content.text: Yes, I have two `xtEnv` constructors in different deftypes. Let me rename one of them. I'll rename the CEnv's one to `xtCEnv`.

edits.oldText:
#|
<cenv> ::= (mtEnv)
          | (xtEnv <symbol> <cvalue> <cenv>)
|#

;; CEnv modela un ambiente para interp donde las variables se
;; asocian a CValues.
(deftype CEnv
  (mtEnv)
  (xtEnv symbol value env))

(define empty-cenv (mtEnv))

;; extend-cenv : symbol CValue CEnv -> CEnv
;; Extiende un ambiente añadiendo una asociación de variable a valor.
(define (extend-cenv x v env)
  (xtEnv x v env))

;; cenv-lookup : symbol CEnv -> (or #f CValue)
;; Busca una variable en un ambiente, retornando el valor asociado
;; o #f si la variable no se encuentra.
(define (cenv-lookup x env)
  (match env
    ((mtEnv) #f)
    ((xtEnv s v tail) (if (symbol=? x s) v (cenv-lookup x tail)))))
edits.newText:
#|
<cenv> ::= (mtCEnv)
          | (xtCEnv <symbol> <cvalue> <cenv>)
|#

;; CEnv modela un ambiente para interp donde las variables se
;; asocian a CValues.
(deftype CEnv
  (mtCEnv)
  (xtCEnv symbol value env))

(define empty-cenv (mtCEnv))

;; extend-cenv : symbol CValue CEnv -> CEnv
;; Extiende un ambiente añadiendo una asociación de variable a valor.
(define (extend-cenv x v env)
  (xtCEnv x v env))

;; cenv-lookup : symbol CEnv -> (or #f CValue)
;; Busca una variable en un ambiente, retornando el valor asociado
;; o #f si la variable no se encuentra.
(define (cenv-lookup x env)
  (match env
    ((mtCEnv) #f)
    ((xtCEnv s v tail) (if (symbol=? x s) v (cenv-lookup x tail)))))

#lang play

#|
Nombre: Tarea 2 CC4101
